pack.name: Embers of the Hold (Sample Expansion)
pack.author: Torchbearer — worked example
pack.description: A worked example of every pack collection: the orc ancestry, a background, shared feats, a new spell, a monster, a companion, and a two-battle mini-adventure.

ancestries.name: Orc
ancestries.desc: Born of hard country and harder history, orcs meet the world chin-first and expect it to blink.

heritages.name: Hold-Scarred Orc
heritages.desc: You wear your survivals proudly. Your maximum HP increases by 2, and pain is an old acquaintance.

heritages.name: Badlands Orc
heritages.desc: Raised under a hateful sun. You gain fire resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1).

heritages.name: Deep Orc
heritages.desc: Cave-born and cave-canny. You are trained in one additional skill of your choice.

backgrounds.name: Hold Smith
backgrounds.desc: You learned that iron only respects patience, and people are mostly iron.

feats.name: Orc Ferocity
feats.desc: Refuse the ground. You are hard to finish, dying with less finality than most.

feats.name: Iron Fists
feats.desc: Your knuckles are a family heirloom. +1 circumstance bonus to initiative when a brawl is clearly coming.

feats.name: Hold Mark
feats.desc: You bear your hold's brand and its obligations. You are trained in Athletics and gain Hold Lore.

feats.name: Furious Focus
feats.desc: Channel fury into precision: a two-handed Power Attack whose miss doesn't count against your multiple attack penalty. (Uses the Power Attack engine hook; flavor differs.)

feats.name: Ember Ward
feats.desc: A smith's respect for fire. You gain fire resistance equal to half your level.

spells.name: Stone Lance
spells.desc: The ground volunteers a spear and you provide the throw.

monsters.name: Cinder Wretch
monsters.desc: What remains when a forge-fire is fed the wrong fuel and remembers it.

monsters.name: The Forge-Tyrant

powers.name: Bellows Blast
powers.flavor: The Tyrant inhales through its ribs and the whole room becomes a forge.

monsters.desc: The hold's master smith, dead a century, still working. It never liked apprentices.

companions.name: Grash One-Tusk
companions.subtitle: Orc Fighter 3
companions.desc: Last apprentice of the old hold. Owes the Tyrant a debt, and intends to pay it in iron.

adventures.name: Embers of the Hold
adventures.blurb: An abandoned orc hold's forge has relit itself, and the smoke smells like grudges. A short two-battle sample adventure.

gates.title: Smoke Over Karn-Buruk
text: The hold of Karn-Buruk has stood empty for a hundred years, which makes the smoke rising from its forge-chimney a matter of professional interest. At the shattered gates, an orc with one tusk and a greataxe watches the smoke the way other people watch a debt collector's door.
"Forge doesn't light itself," Grash says, not looking at you. "And the old master never let a fire go out while there was work owed. I'd call that a hint."

choices.text: Study the gate carvings for the hold's history.
Enter the hold and follow the smoke.

carvings-good.title: The Debt Ledger
text: The gate carvings are a ledger as much as a history: every smith who served, every work-debt owed and paid. The final entry is unfinished — the master's own mark beside a debt with no payment line.
You understand the shape of it now. Whatever works the forge isn't angry. It's owed. Named debts can be settled — and settling them tends to take the fight out of the dead.
(You will begin the final battle with the Forge-Tyrant weakened.)

choices.text: Enter the hold and follow the smoke.

carvings-bad.title: Weathered Past Reading
text: A century of badlands wind has sanded the ledger to suggestion. You learn that orcs lived here, worked iron, and carved very small — none of which was in doubt.
Grash grunts. "Old writing. Old debts. Same thing, mostly."

choices.text: Enter the hold and follow the smoke.

forge-hall-approach.title: The Warming Dark
text: Inside, the hold is cold stone and colder silence — until the third corridor, where the air begins, impossibly, to warm. Ember-light breathes at the far end like a sleeping animal.
Things move in that light. Man-shaped, char-black, glowing at the joints: the old work crew, still on shift.

forge-door.title: The Forge Master's Door
text: Beyond the fallen wretches, the great forge doors stand ajar, breathing heat. Through the gap: a Large shape working an anvil with terrible patience, striking iron that has been finished for a hundred years.
Grash rolls his shoulders. "That's him. That's the master." A pause. "He always said the work outlives the worker. I don't think he meant it as a threat."

choices.text: Enter the forge and end the shift.

epilogue.title: The Fire Banked
text: The Forge-Tyrant comes apart at the joints, ember by ember, and the great fire behind it sinks at last to a bed of quiet coals. Grash stands over the anvil a long moment, then picks up the master's hammer and hangs it on its hook — the small, correct gesture of a shift properly ended.
"Debt's paid," he says. Whether he means the Tyrant's or his own, he doesn't say, and you don't ask.
🔥 THE HOLD SLEEPS — a sample adventure, completed. Now go write a bigger one.

gameover.title: Fed to the Forge
text: The hold is patient and the fire is hungry, and tonight the old master takes on new apprentices.
☠ Your hero has fallen. Forge another — carefully, this time.

enc-corridor.name: The Night Shift
enc-corridor.intro: Char-black figures rise from the ember-light, joints glowing like banked coals. Rubble (difficult terrain) litters the old corridor.

enc-forge.name: The Forge-Tyrant's Shift

knows-debt.log: You name the unpaid debt from the gate ledger as you enter — and the Tyrant's hammer-arm falters, the words settling on it like rust. (Enfeebled 1.)

enc-forge.intro: The forge roars at the Tyrant's back. The anvil island (walls) splits the floor; slag pools glow underfoot (difficult terrain).
